decision: Choose the smallest collaboration topology that can satisfy the task without increasing drift. High-risk work is human-gated; independent specialist work may run as a parallel DAG; ordered specialist work runs as a sequential chain; adversarial review work runs as a debate council.

use_when: one specialist can own the task end to end
later work depends on earlier artifacts
review or approval must happen before delivery
merge or synthesis happens after specialist work completes

subagent_compression.principle: Each subagent runs in an isolated context window and returns a distilled envelope, never the full trace. The orchestrator merges compressed envelopes only.

use_when: adversarial review improves quality beyond self-evaluation
legal review, design critique, or risk assessment requires multiple voices
high-stakes decision benefits from structured disagreement
external side effects or durable memory promotion are possible

drift_controls: Every production run writes a hash-chained run ledger.
Every pause/resume boundary has a checkpoint with state_hash and resume_cursor.
Every cross-Agent handoff has a typed envelope and contract hash.
Every release-critical behavior is covered by deterministic eval cases.
Every subagent in parallel_expert_dag returns a distilled compression envelope, not a full trace.
Every debate_council run records both consensus and dissent with reviewer identities.

content_safety_guardrails.principle: Drift controls ensure process correctness; content safety guardrails ensure the content itself is safe. Both layers are required.
content_safety_guardrails.integration: May be implemented via NeMo Guardrails, OpenAI Agents SDK guardrails, or an equivalent guard layer configured in guardrails.registry.json.
